theater_description: Single-day massacre in the Karantina district of Beirut during the early civil war.
geometry_method_note: Bounded to the Karantina district; no attempt is made to extend this row to wider January 1976 Beirut fighting.
civilian_area_note: Victim pool treated as district residents / camp-adjacent civilians, but no robust population-in-place denominator was found.
split_reason: Separated because Karantina is a discrete massacre episode with its own death range and geography.
quality_note: Death range is widely repeated, but denominator evidence is thin and source base is mostly reference synthesis.

geographic_scope: Tel al-Zaatar camp, East Beirut, Lebanon
theater_description: Siege and final massacre centered on the Tel al-Zaatar Palestinian refugee camp in East Beirut.
geometry_method_note: Bounded to the camp and siege perimeter; wider 1976 Beirut combat is excluded.
military_area_note: The camp contained armed Palestinian fighters, but no defensible combatant person-month denominator was found.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure cannot be cleanly separated from armed-fighter presence with the currently retained sources.
split_reason: Separated because it is a classic siege/massacre episode and should not be merged with Karantina or other Beirut fighting.
quality_note: Direct-death totals are widely cited, but civilian/combatant status separation is unresolved and denominator evidence is poor.

geographic_scope: Sabra neighborhood and Shatila camp, Beirut, Lebanon
theater_description: Three-day massacre in Sabra and Shatila after Israeli forces entered West Beirut and Phalangist forces entered the camps.
geometry_method_note: Bounded to Sabra and Shatila; wider 1982 Lebanon War fatalities are excluded.
military_area_note: No defensible military-exposure denominator identified for the massacre zone.
civilian_area_note: Victim pool is treated as overwhelmingly civilian, but no reliable population-in-place denominator was found for the exact massacre window.
split_reason: Separated because it is the clearest massacre episode in the later Beirut war chronology.
quality_note: The episode is tightly bounded and the civilian character of victims is well supported, but the death range remains broad and denominator-poor.
